dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3832 IS: Small Business Workforce Pipeline Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Rosen (for herself and Mr. Curtis) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship

form.official-title: To amend the Small Business Act to include requirements relating to apprenticeship program assistance for small business development centers, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Small Business Workforce Pipeline Act of 2026.

section.header: Small business development center apprenticeship program assistance
section.text: Section 21(c)(3) of the Small Business Act (15 U.S.C. 648(c)(3)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (T), by striking and at the end;
in clause (v) of the first subparagraph (U) (relating to succession planning), by striking the period at the end and inserting a semicolon;
by redesignating the second subparagraph (U) (relating to training on domestic and international intellectual property protections) as subparagraph (V);
in subparagraph (V)(ii)(II), as so redesignated, by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: providing information and assistance to small business concerns, including by disseminating relevant information from the Department of Labor and other Federal agencies, on how to establish and improve—

clause.text: opportunities for work-based learning, as defined in section 3 of the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006 (20 U.S.C. 2302);
apprenticeship programs registered under the Act of August 16, 1937 (commonly known as the National Apprenticeship Act) (50 Stat. 664, chapter 663; 29 U.S.C. 50 et seq.);
